encryption.description: The network broadcasts without encryption or authentication. Traffic can be captured or modified by anyone within radio range.
encryption.unauthorized_access_scenario: A nearby attacker can join the AP without credentials and observe DNS, HTTP, and other clear-text traffic. They may also attempt man-in-the-middle attacks against connected clients.
encryption.recommendation: Enable WPA3 Personal. If unsupported, use WPA2-AES with a strong random passphrase. Avoid transmitting sensitive data on open hotspots without a VPN.

rogue_ap.description: The SSID 'CoffeeShop_Free' is broadcast by 2 access points (BSSIDs: AA:BB:CC:11:22:33, FF:EE:DD:33:22:11). This may be normal in managed enterprise deployments, but it is suspicious on small or unmanaged networks.
rogue_ap.unauthorized_access_scenario: An attacker can clone a legitimate SSID with a stronger signal. Clients configured to auto-connect may associate with the rogue AP.
rogue_ap.recommendation: Verify each BSSID against a known AP inventory. Remove unapproved devices and use 802.1X or wireless intrusion monitoring for managed environments.

encryption.description: The network broadcasts without encryption or authentication. Traffic can be captured or modified by anyone within radio range.

encryption.recommendation: Enable WPA3 Personal. If unsupported, use WPA2-AES with a strong random passphrase. Avoid transmitting sensitive data on open hotspots without a VPN.

rogue_ap.description: The SSID 'CoffeeShop_Free' is broadcast by 2 access points (BSSIDs: FF:EE:DD:33:22:11, AA:BB:CC:11:22:33). This may be normal in managed enterprise deployments, but it is suspicious on small or unmanaged networks.
rogue_ap.unauthorized_access_scenario: An attacker can clone a legitimate SSID with a stronger signal. Clients configured to auto-connect may associate with the rogue AP.

encryption.description: WEP was deprecated in 2004 and is cryptographically broken. Its RC4-based design allows key recovery from captured traffic.
encryption.unauthorized_access_scenario: An attacker can passively capture enough WEP frames and recover the network key with public tooling, then access the network as an authenticated client.
encryption.recommendation: Replace WEP with WPA3 immediately. Retire devices that only support WEP and avoid sending sensitive data over this network.

encryption.description: WPA-TKIP was a transitional replacement for WEP and is deprecated. It does not meet modern wireless security requirements.
encryption.unauthorized_access_scenario: Attackers may exploit protocol weaknesses or capture authentication handshakes for offline password guessing, especially when passphrases are weak or reused.
encryption.recommendation: Disable WPA and TKIP. Use WPA3 Personal or WPA2-AES with a long random passphrase and current AP firmware.
encryption.wps_warning: WPS is enabled. PIN mode drastically reduces authentication search space and should be disabled on production networks.

configuration.description: The SSID 'Netgear_ABCD' matches a vendor or generic default naming pattern (prefix: 'netgear'). Default naming can reveal device type and may indicate other factory defaults remain unchanged.
configuration.unauthorized_access_scenario: An attacker can infer router vendor or model family, look up common default credentials, and attempt administrative access against the AP.
configuration.recommendation: Use a unique SSID that does not reveal vendor or model details. Change the router admin password and keep firmware updated.

configuration.description: Wi-Fi Protected Setup is enabled. WPS PIN mode has design weaknesses that reduce the practical authentication search space.
configuration.unauthorized_access_scenario: An attacker may attempt online WPS PIN guessing or exploit weak vendor PIN generation to recover the wireless passphrase.
configuration.recommendation: Disable WPS. Replace APs that do not allow WPS to be disabled.

encryption.description: WPA2-AES is broadly secure when paired with a strong passphrase, but captured handshakes can be attacked offline if passwords are weak.
encryption.unauthorized_access_scenario: An attacker who captures a 4-way handshake can run offline dictionary attacks. WPS, default passwords, and short passphrases greatly increase compromise risk.
encryption.recommendation: Use at least 16 random characters, disable WPS, enable Management Frame Protection where supported, and plan a WPA3 upgrade.
encryption.wps_warning: WPS is enabled. PIN mode drastically reduces authentication search space and should be disabled on production networks.

configuration.recommendation: Use a unique SSID that does not reveal vendor or model details. Change the router admin password and keep firmware updated.

configuration.recommendation: Disable WPS. Replace APs that do not allow WPS to be disabled.

configuration.description: The network suppresses its SSID in beacon frames. This offers little practical protection and can increase client probing privacy leakage.
configuration.unauthorized_access_scenario: A passive observer can still identify the hidden network when clients connect or probe for it.
configuration.recommendation: Do not rely on hidden SSIDs as a security control. Use WPA3 or WPA2-AES with a strong passphrase.

encryption.description: WPA3 uses SAE to resist offline dictionary attacks and is the current best practice for personal wireless networks.
encryption.unauthorized_access_scenario: WPA3 materially raises attack cost, but outdated firmware or transition mode can still expose downgrade or implementation risks.
encryption.recommendation: Keep AP firmware current, prefer WPA3-only mode where compatible, and require Management Frame Protection.

configuration.description: The SSID 'HomeNet_5G' matches a vendor or generic default naming pattern (prefix: 'home'). Default naming can reveal device type and may indicate other factory defaults remain unchanged.
configuration.unauthorized_access_scenario: An attacker can infer router vendor or model family, look up common default credentials, and attempt administrative access against the AP.
configuration.recommendation: Use a unique SSID that does not reveal vendor or model details. Change the router admin password and keep firmware updated.
